jokes.text: Looks like we got ourselves an Anus Tart.
jokes.setup: Officers checking license plate of approaching car
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'A NEW START' as 'Anus Tart'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that establishes the central running gag. The mispronunciation is both unexpected and perfectly logical, setting up the episode's theme while being immediately quotable.

jokes.text: Yes, Tobias was finally about to get a starring role on a hit TV show.
jokes.setup: Narrator suggesting Tobias's big break
jokes.punchline: He's about to be featured on 'To Catch a Predator'
jokes.explanation: Dark irony that plays with audience expectations. The narrator's deadpan delivery of this twisted 'achievement' is perfectly structured.

jokes.text: It's Tobias' Arrested Development.
jokes.setup: Parody of the show's own opening credits format
jokes.punchline: The title now refers to Tobias's psychological issues
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke that repurposes the show's title for character-specific meaning. Economical and perfectly integrated with Tobias's storyline.

jokes.text: Everyone thinks I'm gay?
jokes.setup: Tobias shocked to learn family perception
jokes.punchline: His complete obliviousness to obvious gayness
jokes.explanation: Peak character comedy - Tobias's genuine shock at something obvious to everyone else perfectly captures his self-delusion.

jokes.text: ♪ It's just a fallacy! ♪
jokes.setup: Tobias defending his masculinity
jokes.punchline: Immediately singing dramatically to prove he's not gay
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Tobias undermines his own argument immediately with flamboyant musical theater behavior. The irony is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: So he just took Eat and Pray.
jokes.setup: Tobias finding inspiration from 'Eat, Pray, Love'
jokes.punchline: He literally only took the words 'Eat' and 'Pray', leaving 'Love'
jokes.explanation: Clever visual wordplay that literalizes the metaphorical. The economy is perfect - maximum joke density in minimal words.

jokes.text: And these romantic gestures are possibly why people think I'm a homosexual.
jokes.setup: Tobias analyzing why people might think he's gay
jokes.punchline: Blaming romantic gestures instead of obvious gay behaviors
jokes.explanation: Typical Tobias misdirection - he identifies the least gay thing about himself as the problem while remaining oblivious to everything else.

jokes.text: A new start.
jokes.setup: Tobias declaring his fresh beginning
jokes.punchline: Callbacks to the 'Anus Tart' license plate mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that ties together the episode's central wordplay. The phrase gains multiple layers of meaning throughout the episode.

jokes.text: Stay in your lane, anus tart!
jokes.setup: Road rage incident
jokes.punchline: Other drivers also mispronounce the license plate
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that establishes this as a running gag. Multiple people making the same mispronunciation adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: India clean.
jokes.setup: Tobias putting on traditional Indian garb
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'Indian' as 'India clean'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that fits Tobias's character tendency for malapropisms, though not as strong as other examples.

jokes.text: I look like one of those hot guys from Spartacus.
jokes.setup: Tobias admiring himself in makeshift Indian garb
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to attractive gladiators while looking ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias self-delusion - his complete disconnect from how he actually looks provides consistent character comedy.

jokes.text: How could they not have Failure to Launch?
jokes.setup: Tobias complaining about in-flight entertainment
jokes.punchline: His taste in movies reveals character flaws
jokes.explanation: Subtle character work - Tobias's movie preferences reveal his superficiality and poor judgment, though the joke is understated.

jokes.text: This man here broke his skull in two places. Once outside the airport and once in the elevator when his sheet got caught and he was pulled off his gurney. Literally.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining patient's injuries
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous sequence of accidents
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy - each additional injury becomes more absurd. The 'literally' tag from the narrator adds perfect punctuation.

jokes.text: Now I've broken my skull in a third place! On this elephant guy statue!
jokes.setup: Continuation of the skull-breaking pattern
jokes.punchline: Tobias immediately adds another injury
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation that continues the running gag. Tobias's obliviousness to continuing the pattern adds character consistency.

jokes.text: You should be a comedy actor.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving career advice to injured Tobias
jokes.punchline: Suggests comedy based on Tobias's accidental mishaps
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-joke about Tobias being funny without intending to be. The doctor's advice is both sincere and ironic.

jokes.text: It's just, I've got a bit of a stick up my bunghole about what I've now found is a running joke about me.
jokes.setup: Tobias expressing frustration about family jokes
jokes.punchline: Uses gay-coded language while denying gay rumors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - while complaining about gay rumors, he uses incredibly gay-coded language completely unconsciously. Peak character consistency.

jokes.text: For 2,000 rupees, we'd both go down on Matthew McConaughey.
jokes.setup: Discussion of money troubles
jokes.punchline: Lucille's shocking casual prostitution comment
jokes.explanation: Shocking and perfectly in-character for Lucille's amoral pragmatism. The casual delivery makes it even more disturbing/funny.

jokes.text: At the time of this family meeting, 2,000 rupees was $36.
jokes.setup: Lucille's prostitution offer
jokes.punchline: The amount is pathetically small
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator timing - the factual delivery of this devastating detail makes Lucille's comment even more absurd.

jokes.text: I'm a real estate agent. I'm a predator. I sell giant houses to very poor people who can't afford them with predatory loans.
jokes.setup: Tobias thinking he's talking to a talent agent
jokes.punchline: Agent explains he's literally a different kind of predator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on 'predator' with perfect setup/payoff structure. The casual admission of predatory lending practices adds dark humor.

jokes.text: Lindsay! Lindsay, get the scooper! Throw oranges at it. Hot orange! Hot orange! It's throwing its voice! Lindsay!
jokes.setup: Thanksgiving dinner gone wrong
jokes.punchline: Tobias's panicked, nonsensical commands about duck l'orange
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Tobias's characteristic overthinking in a crisis. 'It's throwing its voice' is particularly absurd.

jokes.text: Well, then I shall redouble my efforts so that he died in vain.
jokes.setup: Tobias responding to agent's suicide
jokes.punchline: Misuses 'died in vain' to mean the opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias malapropism - he completely reverses the meaning while sounding confident. Shows his obliviousness to language and social situations.

jokes.text: Meaning he will have led a pointless life, is what I mean.
jokes.setup: Tobias realizing his mistake
jokes.punchline: His clarification makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Tobias's attempt to clarify only makes his insensitivity more explicit. Typical character move.

jokes.text: Get it out there. 'This is up, sir.'
jokes.setup: Tobias giving acting advice
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding addiction confession as acting exercise
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - Tobias treating genuine addiction testimony as performance art shows his complete lack of awareness.

jokes.text: I almost never get recognized as the Invisible Girl.
jokes.setup: DeBrie talking about her acting career
jokes.punchline: Can't get recognized for playing an invisible character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic wordplay - the logical impossibility of being recognized for being invisible is perfectly executed with economy.

jokes.text: This is the gayest Starbucks ever!
jokes.setup: Customer seeing Tobias in his outfit
jokes.punchline: Assumes flamboyant costume means gay coffee shop
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat that plays off Tobias's ridiculous appearance and the ongoing gay jokes, though not as clever as other moments.

jokes.text: She did six of these movies, and after lunch, did three more.
jokes.setup: Describing DeBrie's porn career
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery about prolific same-day production
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing. The 'after lunch' detail makes it even more matter-of-fact and funny.

jokes.text: You remind me of Billy Crystal... Billy Crystal Meth, a funny drug dealer.
jokes.setup: DeBrie apparently complimenting Tobias
jokes.punchline: She's actually referencing a drug dealer
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke - sets up what seems like a genuine compliment then reveals the dark truth. The pun on Billy Crystal is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Did you see my StraightBait movie of the same name?
jokes.setup: Tobias mentions his 'A New Start' license plate
jokes.punchline: DeBrie made a porn movie with same mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that connects multiple plot threads. The coincidence is absurd but perfectly fitting for the show's interconnected storytelling.

jokes.text: Her heart stopped! She's dying! Please, someone! She had too much butter!
jokes.setup: DeBrie collapses during their romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Tobias's panicked explanation involves 'too much butter'
jokes.explanation: Absurd emergency explanation that captures the chaos and Tobias's tendency toward dramatic overstatement.

jokes.text: Someone say 'butter' out here? Oh, my God, that's a butter face.
jokes.setup: Emergency over DeBrie's 'butter' overdose
jokes.punchline: Medical staff making cruel butter face joke
jokes.explanation: Crude wordplay that's more mean-spirited than clever. The double meaning works but lacks the sophistication of better jokes.

jokes.text: Now, what this means in non-medical terms is that you have landed a real party girl.
jokes.setup: Doctor listing DeBrie's numerous STDs
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically calling it 'party girl' status
jokes.explanation: Good understatement - the clinical delivery of this devastating news with casual euphemism creates effective dark humor.

jokes.text: Other people are incapable of ever being nude. Everybody has something.
jokes.setup: Tobias offering perspective on problems
jokes.punchline: Compares drug addiction to his never-nude condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Tobias's attempt at empathy reveals his complete lack of perspective about problem severity. Classic narcissistic comparison.

jokes.text: I almost a guy for a hamburger.
jokes.setup: DeBrie's reaction to turned down job
jokes.punchline: Implies she almost prostituted herself for food
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about desperation. The incomplete sentence leaves the action to imagination while making the implication clear.

jokes.text: I'm Johnny Storm, the Human Flamer.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to clarify their superhero identities
jokes.punchline: Uses gay slur term while denying gayness
jokes.explanation: Another perfect example of Tobias using gay-coded language while trying to appear straight. His obliviousness is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: And this is Sue Storm, my sister and fellow fighter-in-law.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining their relationship
jokes.punchline: Says 'fighter-in-law' instead of proper term
jokes.explanation: Mild malapropism that fits Tobias's tendency to mangle language, though not as memorable as his stronger moments.

jokes.text: Well, apparently in this instance, paper beats rock.
jokes.setup: Getting served legal papers while dressed as The Thing
jokes.punchline: Rock-paper-scissors reference to his rock costume
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures the situation. The rock-paper-scissors metaphor is both apt and economical.

jokes.text: Actually, technically, I'm Rock Monster, and this is Ability to Be Invisible Person.
jokes.setup: Child identifying them as Fantastic Four
jokes.punchline: Tobias's legalistic off-brand superhero names
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - the overly literal, generic superhero names are both legally safe and ridiculous. Fits Tobias's overthinking nature.

jokes.text: No, I was gonna have sex with him. For money.
jokes.setup: Tobias assuming she was selling costume
jokes.punchline: DeBrie clarifies she meant prostitution
jokes.explanation: Dark misdirection that shows how desperate their situation has become. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it more shocking.

jokes.text: Oh, Tobias got a new girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Tobias carrying unconscious Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Buster's misinterpretation of emergency situation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that shows Buster's detachment from reality. His casual misreading of the emergency situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ummmmmm... No.
jokes.setup: Offered $120,000 job while girlfriend considers prostitution
jokes.punchline: Tobias's delayed but definitive rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - the long pause followed by definitive 'no' shows Tobias's stubborn commitment to his delusions despite obvious need.

jokes.text: You're worse than Marky. He couldn't see me, but you can't hear me.
jokes.setup: DeBrie's frustration with Tobias
jokes.punchline: Comparing different types of partner blindness
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison of literal and metaphorical blindness. References previous relationship while highlighting Tobias's selective hearing.

jokes.text: Daddy needs to get his rocks off.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to remove rock costume to see daughter
jokes.punchline: Accidentally uses sexual euphemism on To Catch a Predator
jokes.explanation: Peak Arrested Development moment. Perfect convergence of multiple running gags - Tobias's innocent double entendres, the rock costume, and the predator sting setup. Could become culturally quotable.

jokes.text: I want to say 19? But I like to think of her as 15.
jokes.setup: Asked about his daughter's age on predator show
jokes.punchline: Makes himself sound like pedophile with innocent comment
jokes.explanation: Maximum cringe comedy - Tobias's innocent nostalgia for when his daughter was younger comes across as predatory in this context. Perfect storm of misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I just broke my skull in a fourth place. The patio!
jokes.setup: Continuation of running skull injury gag
jokes.punchline: Fourth injury while trying to see imaginary raccoon
jokes.explanation: Final escalation of the skull injury running gag. Maintains consistency but feels slightly mechanical compared to earlier instances.
